Records.description: Two-loop massless propagator-type integral with five massless internal lines and indices (a1,a2,a3,a4,a5), defined in Eq. (1) and depicted in Fig. 1. The paper specializes the indices to (1/2,1,1/2,1,1), the configuration that arises in the crossed-photon contribution to the two-loop fermion self-energy of RQED_{3,2} (epsilon_e = 1/2, delta_gamma = epsilon_gamma - 1/2 -> 0). Using hypergeometric representations of G(alpha,1,beta,1,1) from Kotikov-Teber (arXiv:1312.2430), the coefficient function G(1/2,1,1/2,1,1) is expanded analytically through O(delta_gamma) (one order beyond finite), yielding poles 1/delta_gamma^2 and 1/delta_gamma plus a finite part involving zeta_2, ln 2, and the Catalan constant G = Cl_2(pi/2); see Eq. (G-RQED32). Substitution into the self-energy then shows that all infrared poles cancel and the two-loop fermion propagator is finite, with the simple gauge-invariant correction -4 pi N_F at O(1).
Records.location: Eq. (1) defines G(a1,a2,a3,a4,a5); the specific case G(1/2,1,1/2,1,1) is computed in Eqs. (sigma-c3)-(G-RQED32) using the representation from Kotikov-Teber arXiv:1312.2430 Eqs. (B10)-(B11).

Records.description: Two-loop massless off-shell self-energy 'master' integral with five massless propagators k^2, (k-p)^2, (k-q)^2, q^2, (q-p)^2, evaluated in D dimensions via the Negative Dimensional Integration Method (NDIM). The result is given in closed form as Gamma functions in D, reproducing the known Chetyrkin-Kataev-Tkachov result via Gegenbauer polynomials. External momentum p is generic (off-shell), no internal masses. The triangle subintegral is treated using a 'shortened' three-Appell-F4 form valid over the full momentum range needed for further loop integration.
Records.familyName: Massless two-loop two-point master self-energy (5 propagators)
Records.location: Section 2, Eq. (1) and (2); Figure 1; final result Eq. between (32) and (33)

Records.description: Two-loop massless propagator-type self-energy diagram J(alpha_1,alpha_2,alpha_3,alpha_4,alpha_5) with five propagators carrying arbitrary (non-integer) indices and a single off-shell external momentum p (Fig. 1). The paper focuses on the specialization J(1,1,1,1,alpha) with arbitrary index alpha on the central line (Fig. 2). Two existing exact (all-orders in eps) representations from the literature (Kazakov 1984 and Kotikov 1996) are shown to be analytically equal: one is a one-fold series equivalent to a sum of two 3F2 hypergeometric functions of argument -1, the other a single 3F2 of argument 1. Several new exact representations involving Psi-functions are derived (Eqs. (Kazakov1), (Kazakov2) and Section 3). Particular cases I(1) and I(1-eps) are recovered as Gamma-function combinations. App. B reproduces the analogous exact results for the related J(alpha,1,beta,1,1) family with two non-adjacent arbitrary indices (same graph topology, different specialization) from Kotikov-Teber 2013.
Records.location: Eq. (1) defines J(alpha_1,...,alpha_5); Eq. (2) defines the central-line specialization J(1,1,1,1,alpha) shown in Fig. 2; existing exact results are Eq. (Kazakov) and Eq. (KotikovG); new representations in Eqs. (Kazakov1), (Kazakov2), and Section 3; the J(alpha,1,beta,1,1) reminder is in App. B (Eqs. (Gab), (I1)-(I4)).

Records.authors: Bargiela, Piotr, Frellesvig, Hjalte, Marzucca, Robin, Morales, Roger, Seefeld, Florian, Wilhelm, Matthias, Yang, Tong-Zhi
Records.description: $I^{**}_{2,1,2}$ two-loop topology with three internal paths of lengths a=2, b=1, c=2 (neither v_L nor v_R carries external legs); 2-point integral, planar (b=1). Geometry: 0 ISPs; algebraic leading singularity; dlog form (§3.4 zero-ISPs)

Names: Massless two-loop two-point master self-energy (5 propagators)
